app.name: Fathom
app.bestFor: Best free AI notetaker — unlimited recording, ~30-second summaries, and the highest G2 rating in the category.
app.rankBasis: The strongest independent review signal in this set: G2 lists Fathom at 5.0/5 from roughly 6,900 reviews, and 2026 meeting-assistant roundups consistently place it among the top picks.
app.pricingFree: Free forever for individuals — unlimited recordings and instant AI summaries
app.pricingPaid: Team $15/user/mo billed annually ($19 monthly; 2-user minimum) — collaboration, playlists, keyword alerts and team controls
pros: exceptionally generous individual free plan
reviewers consistently praise summary quality and ease of setup
large integration ecosystem and strong CRM workflow
cons: team plans require at least two seats
a meeting bot or desktop capture can be undesirable in sensitive calls
advanced collaboration and administration require paid tiers

app.name: Granola
app.bestFor: Best bot-free option — captures audio silently without a visible bot joining the call, ideal for solo users.
app.rankBasis: A frequent top-two pick in 2026 comparisons for bot-free note taking; its independent review footprint is still much smaller than Fathom, with G2 showing only a few dozen reviews.
app.pricingFree: Basic $0/user/mo — limited meeting history, AI meeting notes and cross-meeting chat
app.pricingPaid: Business $14/user/mo — unlimited meeting notes/history, advanced AI models, recipes, integrations and API access
pros: keeps a human-written notepad at the center of the workflow
no visible bot joins the meeting
reviewers praise concise, well-structured notes
cons: smaller independent review base than established competitors
free plan limits meeting history
less suited to teams needing video recordings or sales coaching

app.name: Fireflies.ai
app.bestFor: Best for sales teams — broad CRM and integration coverage with searchable transcript history.
app.rankBasis: A long-running category leader with a 4.7/5 G2 rating from about 750 reviews and repeated placement in 2026 meeting-note roundups.
app.pricingFree: Free forever — unlimited transcription and AI summaries, 400 minutes of team storage and 20 AI credits
app.pricingPaid: Pro $10/user/mo billed annually ($18 monthly) — 8,000 storage minutes/seat, video recording, downloads and API access
pros: broad conferencing and business-app integrations
strong automation for sales and customer-success workflows
unlimited transcription even on the free plan
cons: reviewers report weaker accuracy with accents and noisy audio
AI credits and storage limits add complexity
the meeting bot can be intrusive or blocked by some organizations

app.name: Otter.ai
app.bestFor: Best for real-time live captions during meetings and team transcript collaboration.
app.rankBasis: One of the most established transcription brands, with more than 15 million registered users and a 4.4/5 G2 rating from about 500 reviews, though newer tools now rank above it for summaries.
app.pricingFree: Basic free plan — 300 transcription minutes/mo, 30 minutes per conversation and 3 lifetime file imports
app.pricingPaid: Pro from $8.33/user/mo billed annually — 1,200 transcription minutes/mo and 90 minutes per conversation
features: AI meeting summaries and action items
pros: mature real-time transcription experience
widely used across meetings, interviews and education
good collaborative transcript editing and search
cons: free tier has strict monthly and per-meeting limits
accuracy drops with overlapping speech, accents or poor audio
users report occasional bot-permission and subscription friction

app.name: Fellow
app.bestFor: Full meeting lifecycle — agendas before, notes during, and AI follow-ups after; a Wirecutter top pick.
app.rankBasis: A recurring team-workflow pick in 2026 roundups and a security-focused choice with a substantial established G2 review base, though it is less dominant for individual note taking.
app.pricingFree: Free plan available — core collaborative meeting notes and limited AI meeting assistance
app.pricingPaid: Paid tiers available; current public page does not expose a stable self-serve price in static content
pros: combines preparation, notes and follow-through in one workspace
strong privacy, administration and team collaboration controls
reviewers value templates and recurring-meeting workflows
cons: heavier workflow than a simple personal transcript tool
best value depends on broad team adoption
pricing and plan boundaries are less transparent than several rivals

app.name: tl;dv
app.bestFor: Best free tier for teams — unlimited video, transcripts, and AI summaries across Zoom, Meet, and Teams.
app.rankBasis: A well-reviewed remote-team option with roughly 500 G2 reviews and repeated 2026 roundup inclusion, especially for clips and multi-meeting analysis.
app.pricingFree: Free forever — unlimited meetings/viewers and recording/transcription, plus limited AI notes, queries and reports
app.pricingPaid: Pro $18/user/mo — unlimited AI notes, Ask tl;dv, multi-meeting reports and uploads, plus 5,000+ integrations
pros: feature-rich free plan with unlimited recordings
strong clips, sharing and cross-meeting analysis
reviewers praise transcription and reusable note templates
cons: business plan is a steep price jump for coaching features
mobile experience trails its desktop workflow
the range of controls can feel complex for individual users

app.name: Jamie
app.bestFor: Best for privacy — bot-free, on-device capture that keeps recordings off the cloud.
app.rankBasis: A differentiated bot-free option appearing in multiple 2026 comparisons, but with a thinner independent review footprint than the top six products.
app.pricingFree: Free — 10 meetings/mo with a 30-minute meeting limit
app.pricingPaid: Plus €21/mo billed annually — 20 meetings/mo, 2-hour meetings and productivity-app exports
features: AI summaries, action items and templates
pros: works across online and in-person meetings without a visible bot
editable transcripts and remembered speaker labels
privacy-focused desktop capture
cons: free and entry paid plans impose meeting-count limits
smaller community and integration ecosystem than category leaders
unlimited use starts on the considerably more expensive Pro tier

skill.name: Meetily
skill.bestFor: The recognized #1 self-hosted, open-source meeting notetaker (26.4k★) — local Parakeet/Whisper live transcription, speaker diarization, and Ollama summarization, 100% on-device with no cloud.
skill.rankBasis: The leading purpose-built self-hosted meeting assistant here at about 26.4k GitHub stars, with active 2026 development and repeated self-hosted roundup coverage.
features: Desktop apps for macOS and Windows
pros: meeting audio can remain entirely on-device
complete note-taking workflow rather than only a speech model
active project with a large self-hosting community
cons: local models need meaningful CPU, GPU and storage resources
setup and model management are more involved than SaaS
fewer polished integrations and admin controls than commercial tools

skill.name: Whisper (OpenAI)
skill.bestFor: The open-source transcription foundation (105k★) behind most local meeting-notes stacks — accurate, multilingual, and fully offline. The engine, not a full notetaker.
skill.rankBasis: The category's foundational open-source speech model, with about 105.5k GitHub stars and extensive downstream adoption across transcription tools.
pros: widely tested model with broad language support
large ecosystem of ports, wrappers and tutorials
runs locally without sending audio to a service
cons: not a complete meeting-notes application
large models are slow and memory-intensive without a GPU
no built-in diarization or polished real-time workflow

skill.name: whisper.cpp
skill.bestFor: The high-performance C/C++ port of Whisper (52.2k★) — fast, dependency-light on-device transcription that powers Meetily and many local meeting-notes tools.
skill.rankBasis: The dominant lightweight Whisper runtime at about 52.3k GitHub stars, widely recommended when local speed, portability and CPU execution matter.
pros: portable with minimal runtime dependencies
efficient quantized inference on laptops and edge devices
broad hardware and platform support
cons: still requires users to build the meeting workflow around it
quality and speed depend heavily on model and hardware choices
speaker diarization and summarization are not core features

skill.name: WhisperX
skill.bestFor: Whisper plus accurate word-level timestamps and speaker diarization (23.2k★) — the who-said-what layer that makes raw transcripts usable as meeting minutes.
skill.rankBasis: A heavily adopted Whisper enhancement with about 23.2k GitHub stars, known in transcription communities for word-level alignment and diarization.
pros: more precise timestamps than base Whisper
useful speaker-aware output for meeting pipelines
substantial speed gains through batching
cons: installation spans several models and dependencies
best performance generally requires a CUDA-capable GPU
diarization may require external model access and tuning

skill.name: meeting-notes (claude-office-skills)
skill.bestFor: A dedicated MIT-licensed Claude skill that turns a raw transcript into structured minutes — decisions, owned action items, and a recap. Small community repo (~326★), so ranked mid.
skill.rankBasis: A focused office-work skill in a small but relevant repository of about 327 GitHub stars, also indexed by multiple Claude skill catalogs; independent usage evidence remains limited.
pros: purpose-built output structure for business minutes
easy to inspect and customize as plain instructions
fits existing Claude skill workflows
cons: does not record or transcribe audio by itself
quality depends on the supplied transcript and model
small repository and limited independent user feedback

skill.name: meeting-insights-analyzer (ComposioHQ)
skill.bestFor: A meeting-insights skill from Composio's 69.7k-star skills collection — extracts themes, decisions, speaking ratios, and follow-ups from transcripts.
skill.rankBasis: Hosted in the highly visible ComposioHQ skill collection at about 69.8k GitHub stars, but the individual analyzer has no separate adoption count or substantial independent review base.
pros: goes beyond minutes into reusable meeting insights
transparent, editable skill instructions
easy to combine with other agent workflows
cons: parent-repository stars do not measure this skill's usage
requires a transcript and a compatible agent runtime
no dedicated UI, capture pipeline or independent review corpus

notes: Skill/Repo track is ranked by real quality, reputation, and adoption — not form factor; kind (skill|repo) is only a tag. Meetily (26.4k★) leads as the recognized #1 self-hosted, purpose-built meeting notetaker; the massively adopted transcription engines Whisper (105k★), whisper.cpp (52.2k★), and WhisperX (23.2k★, diarization) produce the transcript most stacks are built on. The small community Agent Skills that turn a transcript into structured minutes (claude-office-skills meeting-notes ~326★, ComposioHQ meeting-insights-analyzer) fill the mid tier — genuine but low-adoption, so they no longer sit above the far larger repos. No MCP servers. Star counts verified on GitHub. App consensus is strong: Fathom, Granola, Fireflies, and Otter recur across nearly every independent list; some review sites (tldv, Jamie, alfred) are vendors ranking within their own comparisons.
